notFound.title: Page not found.
notFound.desc: The page you are looking for does not exist or may have been moved. Let's get you back on track.

hero.badge: Next.js · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS · Arabic + English
hero.sub: ORAX is a modern SaaS starter system built for real products, with first-class RTL support, clean architecture, and a bilingual foundation from day one.

logos.label: Built with the tools you already love

perf.label: Performance
perf.title: Lighthouse 95+
perf.desc: PPR, optimized fonts, lazy images. Ships fast out of the box.

theme.label: Dark / Light Mode
theme.title: Pixel-perfect in both themes
theme.desc: Full CSS Variables system. localStorage persistence. Smooth transitions.

anim.label: Cinematic Animations
anim.title: GSAP + Framer Motion — scroll-driven storytelling
anim.desc: Page load sequences, ScrollTrigger reveals, custom cursor, particle canvas. Every animation is purposeful.

dx.label: Developer Experience
dx.title: TypeScript strict mode, fully typed
dx.desc: Every component documented. Biome for linting. Zero guesswork.

tokens.label: Design Tokens
tokens.title: Brand it in 30 seconds
tokens.desc: Change your brand color once in globals.css and the entire template updates.

rtl.label: RTL Foundation
rtl.title: Arabic and RTL are built in from the start
rtl.desc: ORAX is not retrofitted for Arabic. Direction, spacing, typography, and layout behavior are treated as part of the system.

structure.label: Clean Structure
structure.title: Clear separation between UI, system, and future app logic
structure.desc: The project is shaped to avoid mixing landing concerns with future SaaS logic, making the codebase easier to grow.

free.label: Free Edition
free.title: A polished free version that still feels complete
free.desc: ORAX Free is designed to build trust through real UI quality, not through fake backend promises.

pro.label: Pro Direction
pro.title: A real path toward a production SaaS system
pro.desc: ORAX Pro is positioned as the technical foundation for real authentication, protected routes, APIs, and scalable product architecture.

design.label: Design System
design.title: A consistent interface language across the product
design.desc: Shared tokens, reusable patterns, and visual consistency make the UI easier to maintain and extend.

positioning.label: Product Thinking
positioning.title: Built for serious SaaS builders targeting Arabic users
positioning.desc: ORAX is intentionally shaped for developers who need bilingual product credibility, not another generic starter.

how.label: 05 — Process
how.heading: From install to
how.sub: No complex setup. No server configurations. Just clone, fill your .env, and deploy.

s1.title: Clone and install with pnpm
s1.after: inside the project. TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Biome, shadcn/ui — all pre-configured.

s2.title: Fill in your .env.local
s2.after: and add your API keys. Every variable is documented.

s3.title: Brand it in 30 seconds
s3.after: and everything updates. RTL flips with one attribute.

s4.title: Push to Vercel, go live
s4.after: or connect your GitHub repo. Live in under 2 minutes.

editions.label: 03 — Editions
editions.heading: Choose the right
editions.sub: ORAX is now structured into two clear editions: a polished free UI system and a real Pro SaaS foundation.

free.name: ORAX Free
free.desc: A complete free interface system to explore the ORAX approach, design language, RTL support, and product structure.
free.note: Best for evaluation, inspiration, and audience building.

pro.name: ORAX Pro
pro.desc: The real SaaS foundation with authentication, protected routes, backend structure, and scalable architecture for actual products.
pro.note: Built for serious SaaS products and long-term system growth.

faq.label: 04 — FAQ
faq.heading: Questions worth
faq.sub: The goal is clarity: what ORAX is, what it includes, and how Free and Pro are intentionally separated.
faq.q1: Is ORAX Free just a teaser?
faq.a1: No. ORAX Free is meant to feel complete from a UI and product experience perspective. It is designed to build trust, not to feel broken on purpose.
faq.q2: What is the actual difference between Free and Pro?
faq.a2: Free focuses on polished UI, landing, auth screens, dashboard views, settings screens, RTL support, and the design system. Pro is the real SaaS system layer with authentication, protected routes, API structure, database integration, and scalable architecture.
faq.q3: Does ORAX really support Arabic and RTL properly?
faq.a3: Yes. Arabic and RTL are part of the foundation, not an afterthought. Layout direction, spacing, typography, and interface behavior are considered as part of the system design.
faq.a4: It is built for serious developers and SaaS builders who need a bilingual foundation, real RTL credibility, and a codebase that can grow cleanly.
faq.q5: Is ORAX trying to be a generic starter for everyone?
faq.a5: No. ORAX is intentionally opinionated. Its value comes from focus: Arabic support, clean structure, and a more system-oriented way of building SaaS products.
faq.q6: Why split the product into Free and Pro?
faq.a6: Because mixing polished UI and real backend power into one unclear package creates confusion. The split keeps Free visually complete and Pro technically meaningful.

cta.label: 05 — Next Step
cta.sub: ORAX is being shaped as a serious SaaS system: clear in its Free edition, meaningful in its Pro direction, and opinionated where it should be.
cta.note: Real RTL support · Bilingual foundation · Clean architecture mindset

footer.tagline: A serious SaaS starter system built for Arabic credibility, RTL support, and cleaner product foundations.
footer.author: Omar Mahmoud Ahmad
